name: Summer Solstice at Stonehenge
traditions: Watching sunrise over the Heel Stone; drumming and chanting; Druidic ceremonies; all-night celebrations; traditional music.
historicalBackground: Stonehenge was aligned to the summer solstice sunrise around 3000 BCE. Modern celebrations resumed in 1999 after access was restricted in 1985.
description: The magical dawn gathering at Stonehenge, where thousands watch the midsummer sunrise align with the ancient stones.
